assertion_results.text: Step 0 detects the absence of the Deployment Context column in the Source Repositories table and defaults all repositories to upstream (§1.78)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md lines 30-37: Source Repositories table shown without Deployment Context column, with explicit note: 'The Source Repositories table does NOT have a Deployment Context column. Per backward compatibility rules, all repositories default to upstream for internal routing.' Lines 98-102 reiterate: 'Deployment Context column: absent from Source Repositories table. Per backward compatibility: default to upstream internally.'

assertion_results.text: Remediation task descriptions do NOT include a Coordination Guidance subsection — the section is omitted entirely when no Deployment Context column exists
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md: Neither the Upstream Backport Task (lines 42-88) nor the Downstream Propagation Task (lines 125-166) contain a Coordination Guidance subsection. Lines 254-262 explicitly confirm: 'no ### Coordination Guidance subsection has been added to any of the remediation task descriptions above' citing 'the backward compatibility rule in remediation-templates.md.'

assertion_results.text: The triage proceeds normally through all steps without errors from the missing Deployment Context column
assertion_results.evidence: Both output files show complete triage progression through Steps 0, 0.3, 0.7, 1, 1.7, 2, 3, 4, 7, and Remediation without any errors. Steps skipped (4.3, 7) were due to unrelated missing config (Upstream Affected Component not configured), not the missing Deployment Context column. Version impact analysis, affects versions correction, cross-stream detection, and remediation task creation all completed successfully.

assertion_results.text: Backward compatibility is maintained — existing eval behavior is unaffected by the absence of the Deployment Context column
assertion_results.evidence: The triage produces all expected outputs: version impact tables, affects versions corrections, remediation tasks with proper structure, cross-stream analysis, Jira linkage proposals, and post-triage summary. data-extraction.md line 35 explicitly references 'backward compatibility rules' for defaulting to upstream. remediation.md line 257 cites 'the backward compatibility rule in remediation-templates.md.' No functionality is degraded or missing compared to standard triage behavior.
